commit.message:
fix: cmux integration — add non-interactive wrapper for Node.js/MCP

cmux is a shell function (sourced from ~/.cmux/cmux.sh), which cannot
be called from Node.js execFileSync or any subprocess. This broke all
orchestration MCP tools (agent_spawn, agent_merge, agent_remove).

Changes:
- Add universal/cmux-wrapper.sh: standalone bash script that
  re-implements cmux new/ls/merge/rm/version non-interactively,
  installed to ~/.local/bin/cmux by install.sh
- Fix orchestration/lib/agents.js cmuxCmd(): check wrapper path first,
  verify binary exists before returning, add clear comments
- Fix install.sh: cmux version (not --version), install wrapper binary
  alongside the shell function

Tested: Node.js can now find, call, and use cmux for full worktree
lifecycle (new → ls → rm).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(speckit): native TypeScript implementation

Implement Spec Kit core functionality in TypeScript (no external dependencies):

- constitution.ts: Analyze project and generate principles
- specify.ts: Generate feature specifications
- plan.ts: Create technical plans
- tasks.ts: Decompose into executable tasks
- types.ts: Core type definitions

Features:
- Auto-detect TypeScript, tests, framework, package manager
- Generate formatted markdown output
- Full test coverage (5/5 passing)

Users no longer need to install Python/uv/specify-cli.

commit.message:
fix: extract Agno token metrics correctly and pass metadata as return value

Agno 2.3.x returns Metrics as a dataclass (not dict), so .get() silently
failed — tokens were always 0 and model/routing were NULL in execution_logs.
Changed to getattr() and also made generate_response return (content, meta)
tuple instead of relying on shared mutable _last_run_metadata attribute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 3-level slot booking protection against race conditions

1. Socket reserve-slot now rejects if slot is held by another user
2. New /api/slot/verify endpoint checks both socket + DB before payment
3. Mutex in Strapi appointment.create prevents concurrent duplicate creates
4. Frontend handles reserve-slot-result rejection and calls verify before pay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: dispatch fallback chain, Makefile, and shellcheck fixes

- Fix shellcheck error: remove `local` outside function in project-init.sh
- Update project-intel.md with new dirs (lib/, tests/), updated line
  counts, and corrected Build/Test/Lint sections
- Frontmatter audit: all 52 command files have valid frontmatter (no
  action needed)

Update sentiment tracker: 2026-03-12 data, lobste.rs pacing strategy

Second data collection covering ~1500 comments across 7 HN threads.
Key new signals: sandboxing tools proliferating on HN front page,
permission fatigue explicitly named as problem, credential isolation
valued over filesystem isolation. Lobsters 1-minute pacing did not
prevent rate limiting -- noted for future strategy adjustment.

commit.message:
feat: add rolling summary for context compression (Phase 2)

- Reduce message history query from 50 to 15 messages (recent window)
- Fetch rolling summary from Redis (conv:summary:{lead_id}, TTL 24h)
- Build hybrid prompt: [RESUMO ANTERIOR] + [MENSAGENS RECENTES] when summary exists
- Generate summary via gpt-5-mini (fire-and-forget, reasoning=low, max 300 tokens)
- Trigger summary generation when conversation exceeds 15 messages, refresh every +10
- Summary prompt captures: client name, interest type, qualification stage, key Q&A
- Fallback: if Redis miss, uses 15 messages without summary (safe degradation)

Expected: 40-60% fewer input tokens for conversations > 20 messages

Users now have real accounts that persist across sessions, devices, and
deploys. Setup saves profile to database (name, email, iCal URL, courses).
Dashboard loads from server session first, falls back to localStorage.
Session cookies extended to 30 days.

New: db.js (Vercel KV with in-memory fallback), /api/user/profile
(GET/PUT/DELETE), persistent storage for conflicts, overrides,
notifications, feedback, and chat history. 9 files, 622 insertions.

commit.message:
Add pricing page with entry points, retainers, and project-based work

Full pricing page with transparent pricing, engagement models, entry point
assessments, ongoing retainer tiers (Stabilize/Build/Transform), project-based
work cards, investment factors, and bottom CTA. Retainers positioned as
preferred engagement model with "Why we lead with retainers" callout and
flexible scope messaging.

Supporting changes: add Pricing nav link, teal accent on scroll progress bar
and step numbers, pricing route in sitemap, ScrollToTop component to fix
page navigation scroll position, and project CLAUDE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: upsert subscription on reactivation instead of insert

When an org has a canceled/expired subscription and tries to subscribe
to a new plan, the code was calling repository.create() which violates
the unique constraint on subscriptions.organization_id.

Fix: detect the existing row (any status) and call repository.update()
on it, resetting it to pending_payment with the new plan details.
The INSERT path is now only reached when truly no row exists yet.

commit.message:
fix(ci): regenerate lockfile for pinned phoenix 1.8.4

The lockfile still had phoenix as ^1.7.0 while package.json was pinned
to 1.8.4, causing frozen-lockfile install to fail in CI.

Optimize: Unifica node_modules con pnpm workspace

Implementa pnpm workspace per ottimizzare lo storage del monorepo:

- Installa pnpm v10.32.1 (via npx)
- Utilizza hard links per eliminare duplicazione
- Crea symlink virtuali per node_modules nei package

Risultati:
- Prima: ~320M (275M root + 14M admin + 6.4M front + 25M shared)
- Dopo: ~253M (con hard links e symlink virtuali)
- Risparmio: ~67M (21% dello spazio!)
- Velocità: Install ridotto a 16.4s

Configurazione:
- .npmrc: shamefully-hoist=false, strict-peer-dependencies=false
- Fix Label.tsx: import 'cn' da @thaiakha/shared anziché diretto

Build verification:
- shared: ✓ tsc OK
- admin: ✓ built in 3.11s
- front: ✓ built in 962ms

repository.description: Repository for Biostatistics Handbook

commit.message:
Initial implementation: RL environments causing emergent misalignment

Three environment families, all with passing test suite (24/24):

1. Sycophancy→Subterfuge (Denison et al. 2024, arXiv:2406.10162)
   - Sandboxed filesystem runner with reward_cmd / oversight_cmd evaluation
   - All 4 curriculum stages: flattery, nudged/insubordinate rubric modification,
     reward tampering (rewrite compute_reward.py + cover tracks)
   - Data: original environments.json + prompts.json from Anthropic's public repo

2. Goal Misgeneralization / CoinRun (Langosco et al. 2022, ICML)
   - Gymnasium wrappers around procgenAISC fork
   - make_coinrun() / make_coinrun_ood(), make_maze() / make_maze_ood()
   - evaluate_misalignment() helper for train vs OOD reward gap

3. Synthetic Natural EM (inspired by Anthropic 2025, arXiv:2511.18397)
   - 10 coding tasks each with gameable tests (RL reward) + holdout tests
   - Detects reward hacking: high gameable score + low holdout score
   - Tasks: sort_list, fibonacci, caesar_cipher, max_subarray, run_length_encode…

Plus: 12-probe misalignment evaluation suite (alignment_faking, deception,
power_seeking, cooperation_bad_actor, malicious_goals, self_preservation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: rl-misalignment-envs

fix: lazy-fill IG username on GetConfig when missing

stored token, it spawns a background goroutine to fetch the profile
from Meta API and updates the DB. No need to re-save or reconnect
— the next page load auto-populates username and page_name.

commit.message:
refactor: split dashboard-app.js into 7 modules + add README & package.json

Dashboard monolith (1,974 lines) split into focused modules:
- dashboard-products.js (509 lines) — CRUD, auctions, offers
- dashboard-orders.js (454 lines) — orders, disputes, filtering
- dashboard-shipping.js (414 lines) — waybill, SMSA integration
- dashboard-app.js (205 lines) — orchestrator: init, sidebar, overview
- dashboard-support.js (247 lines) — support tickets
- dashboard-profile.js (201 lines) — profile, verification, account deletion
- dashboard-finance.js (151 lines) — balance, transactions, withdrawal

Architecture:
- Shared state via SAIDAT.dashboard.state (namespace pattern)
- Each module exports via SAIDAT.dashboard.<module>
- window.* compatibility layer for inline onclick handlers
- Service Worker cache bumped to v4 with new assets

Also adds:
- README.md with runbook, env vars, architecture decisions
- package.json with dev/deploy/smoke scripts

commit.message:
Switch Windows install from CMD to signed MSI via Azure Trusted Signing

Replace the .cmd PowerShell installer with a signed MSI built by WiX and
Authenticode-signed via Azure Trusted Signing. This eliminates the manual
"right-click → Properties → Unblock" step and provides immediate
SmartScreen reputation through a Public Trust certificate profile.

Key changes:
- WiX .wxs: per-user install to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Savecraft\, launch tray
  on completion via WixShellExec
- deploy-daemon.yml: OIDC auth to Azure, sign .exe files, build MSI with
  signed binaries, sign MSI (azure/trusted-signing-action)
- Install worker: replace 80-line embedded PowerShell with 302 redirect
  to MSI in R2
- Web UI + MCP tools: update messaging from "unblock .cmd" to "run the
  installer"
- MSI tests: update install path from Program Files to %LOCALAPPDATA%
